mode_selection_rationale: Pre-flight directive scan: NO pending directives (47 directives in dir, all status=completed/open/superseded). Mode 1 quick-checks GREEN: all live agents fresh (analyst 0.7h ago, decider 0.5h, curmudgeon 0.1h, workspace-sync 0.07h, integrity 1.6h, analyst-baby 2.9h, curmudgeon-verify 3.1h, poller 3.6h). 6 stranded-patches files total, 5 tombstoned (skipped), 0 active-aged. Mode 2 quick-checks GREEN: auth=200, FUSE staleness — 1 of 10 files (closed-issues.json) 4h behind git, but this is expected for decider-pushed git-owned files awaiting next build.js publish round-trip. Disk df=99% but PROJ_MB=1380 is dominated by FUSE workspace baseline (732MB, fixed) + this tinker run's own /tmp/edit-clone (640MB, ephemeral). No /tmp/*-clone older than 24h eligible for cleanup empowerment. -> Pipeline healthy -> Mode 3 (default productive state).

details.freshness_evaluation: All agents within expected cadence band. sloppytoppy-score 24h matches daily cron; sloppytoppy-rewrite 30.6h matches every-2-days cron AND enabled=false (operator-paused pending F-category architecture audit). Anomaly: sloppytoppy-score lastRunAt=2026-05-17T02:31Z but nextRunAt=2026-05-19T02:30Z — scheduler claims today's 03:30-local fire is being skipped. Logged for next-run follow-up.

details.fuse_staleness_check: 1 of 10 hashed files mismatched (monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json). Diff = last_verify_attempt timestamps 4h behind. Expected behavior for git-owned files written by decider's clone; FUSE catches up on next build.js publish.

disk_check.breakdown: FUSE workspace 732MB baseline + /tmp/edit-clone 640MB ephemeral + /tmp/dome-*.html ~1MB. No clones older than 24h.

mode_4_proposals_and_fixes.reason: Mode 3 selected; no new PROPs authored this run.

Note: proposal file status='operator-approved' lags actual application — housekeeping nit, not a defect.

findings.title: analyst-baby running 4h cadence on drained BAU backlog
findings.summary: dome-analyst-baby (Sonnet, 4h) latest run was NO-OP. assigned-analyst=2 well under operator's 5-item re-bump threshold. Likely no-op for many consecutive runs since 2026-05-13~14 burndown. Sonnet is cheap so urgency is low, but throttling 4h -> 8h/12h would eliminate pure no-op runs.
findings.actionable_by: operator (already owns this knob per scheduler description)
findings.recommendation: No PROP. Operator may flip dome-analyst-baby cron to 0 */8 * * * or 0 */12 * * * and re-bump on assigned-analyst > 5.

May resolve naturally; or one-cycle scheduler skip. Phase 2 rewriter operator-paused (enabled=false) so a one-day scoring gap doesn't propagate.
